name: Arizona Dept of Administration (ADOA-ASET)
tagline: Arizona's central IT and procurement hub: ADOA houses the ASET office (the statewide CIO function) and the State Procurement Office, so it sets technology strategy, runs statewide contracts, and gates high-risk IT projects for state agencies.
overview: The Arizona Department of Administration (ADOA) is the state's central support agency, and two of its offices matter most to an IT seller: the Arizona Strategic Enterprise Technology (ASET) office and the State Procurement Office (SPO). ASET is Arizona's statewide IT authority — it sets technology strategy, policy and standards, delivers shared enterprise services, and oversees high-risk technology projects across state agencies through the IT Authorization Committee (ITAC) and the Project Investment Justification (PIJ) process. State CIO J.R. Sloan leads ASET and is the top relationship for the statewide motion; he is best known for Arizona's Cloud-First Policy, which has driven the closure of roughly 90 on-premise data centers and migration of over 2,900 applications. SPO, also under ADOA, runs the statewide contracts and the Arizona State Purchasing Cooperative that local governments, schools and nonprofits buy through. ADOA is more conduit and oversight body than large end-spender: the biggest agency IT budgets sit with the agencies themselves, but ADOA shapes the policy, the vehicles, and the project approvals those buys flow through.

budget.intro: ADOA-ASET's leverage is policy, oversight and procurement rather than a large discretionary IT appropriation. ASET reviews and oversees agency technology projects statewide via ITAC/PIJ, while the dollars for most projects sit in the sponsoring agencies' budgets. Treat figures below as directional and confirm against the latest Arizona executive budget and JLBC documents.

stats.label: Project oversight gate
stats.note: ASET reviews and approves high-risk agency IT projects

notes: ASET recovers costs for shared enterprise services; the largest IT line items live in the sponsoring agencies, not ADOA.
PIJ Portal usage rose sharply (a cited ~290% increase FY2019–FY2024), driving added ASET oversight capacity in recent budget requests.
Always validate dollar figures against the current azospb.gov executive budget and azjlbc.gov appropriations reports before forecasting.

initiatives.name: Cloud-First consolidation
initiatives.summary: Arizona's long-running Cloud-First Policy (closing on-premise data centers, migrating apps to cloud and SaaS) remains a core ASET program. Position infrastructure, hybrid-cloud and migration offerings around the next wave of workloads.

initiatives.name: Generative AI policy & adoption
initiatives.summary: ASET maintains a statewide Generative AI policy (P2000), the state has rolled out employee Gen AI training, and Governor Hobbs stood up an AI Steering Committee. AI governance, data readiness and responsible adoption are active priorities — tie AI-infrastructure plays to the policy guardrails.

initiatives.name: Statewide data strategy
initiatives.summary: Arizona's CIO, CISO and chief data officer are jointly driving a statewide data strategy emphasizing data governance and readiness — a foundation for AI and analytics workloads.

initiatives.name: Statewide cybersecurity & cyber readiness
initiatives.summary: ASET sets statewide security policy and standards; a Statewide Cyber Readiness program extends resources to local and tribal governments. Security and Zero Trust modernization run alongside the cloud and AI tracks.

procurement.intro: Selling into Arizona state government runs on the State Procurement Office's statewide contracts and the State Purchasing Cooperative, plus ASET's project-oversight gate. Confirm which contracts Dell holds, and remember that high-risk technology buys must clear the ITAC/PIJ review before they proceed.

vehicles.name: Arizona statewide contracts (SPO)
vehicles.detail: ADOA's State Procurement Office establishes statewide contracts for use by all state agencies and eligible cooperative members — the primary path for technology equipment and services.

vehicles.name: Arizona State Purchasing Cooperative
vehicles.detail: Extends state contracts to 900+ political subdivisions, schools and nonprofits — the route to county, city, K-12 and higher-ed buyers across the state.

vehicles.name: Central vs. agency procurement
vehicles.detail: SPO runs statewide vehicles centrally, but individual agencies also run their own delegated procurements — confirm whether a given opportunity is central (SPO) or agency-led.

vehicles.name: ITAC / PIJ oversight
vehicles.detail: High-risk agency IT projects must pass ASET's Project Investment Justification and IT Authorization Committee review — a gate to map early on larger deals.

powerStructure.name: J.R. Sloan
powerStructure.title: State CIO / Director, ASET
powerStructure.note: Owns statewide IT strategy, governance and shared services; cloud-first and AI-forward. The top relationship for the ADOA-ASET motion.

powerStructure.name: ADOA Director
powerStructure.title: Department Director
powerStructure.note: Cabinet-level head of ADOA (a Hobbs appointee); ASET and SPO both report up through the department. Confirm the current named director before outreach.

powerStructure.name: State CISO
powerStructure.title: Chief Information Security Officer
powerStructure.note: Sets statewide security policy and standards (Ryan Murray has held the state CISO role, dual-hatted with Homeland Security). Confirm current incumbent — the security buying center to map.

powerStructure.name: Chief Data Officer
powerStructure.title: Statewide data strategy
powerStructure.note: Co-leads the statewide data strategy with the CIO and CISO — relevant for data-readiness and AI foundations.

powerStructure.name: State Procurement Administrator
powerStructure.title: Head of the State Procurement Office
powerStructure.note: Owns statewide contracts and the purchasing cooperative — the procurement path onto Arizona public-sector buys.

news.title: State Procurement Office files expedited rulemaking for ADOA-SPO procurement rules
news.impact: Procurement rule changes underway (Title 2, Ch. 7) — watch for shifts in solicitation and cooperative-purchasing terms.

news.title: ASET's IT Authorization Committee (ITAC) reviews statewide agency IT projects, chaired by CIO J.R. Sloan
news.impact: Confirms the active project-oversight gate; ITAC approvals are a forecasting signal for agency technology buys.

news.title: Arizona updates statewide Generative AI policy (P2000)
news.impact: Refreshed AI guardrails emphasize data governance, security and transparency — align AI offerings to the policy.

news.title: Hobbs enters the legislative session with several unconfirmed agency directors
news.impact: Leadership confirmation churn across agencies — verify the current ADOA director status before executive outreach.

dellActions: Build the top relationship with State CIO J.R. Sloan and the ASET team — they own statewide strategy, shared services and the project-oversight gate.
Confirm Dell's coverage on Arizona statewide contracts (SPO) and cooperative eligibility — it's the access path to state agencies plus 900+ co-op members.
Lead cloud-first modernization and AI-infrastructure offerings tied to the Cloud-First Policy and the statewide data strategy / Gen AI policy guardrails.
Map the ITAC/PIJ oversight process early on larger deals so agency projects clear ASET review without surprises.
Verify current named leadership (ADOA Director, State CISO) before executive outreach, given recent confirmation and personnel churn.
